name: Rectus Abdominis
origin: Pubic crest and pubic symphysis
insertion: Cartilage of ribs 5-7 and xiphoid process
action: Flexes vertebral column; tilts pelvis posteriorly; compresses abdominal contents

name: External Oblique
origin: External surfaces of ribs 5-12
insertion: Anterior iliac crest; rectus sheath to linea alba
action: Bilaterally: flexes vertebral column, compresses abdomen. Unilaterally: laterally flexes to same side, rotates to OPPOSITE side

name: Internal Oblique
origin: Lateral inguinal ligament, iliac crest, thoracolumbar fascia
insertion: Internal surface of lower 3 ribs; rectus sheath to linea alba
action: Bilaterally: flexes vertebral column, compresses abdomen. Unilaterally: laterally flexes to same side, rotates to SAME side

name: Erector Spinae
origin: Posterior iliac crest, sacrum, spinous and transverse processes of vertebrae, posterior ribs
insertion: Ribs, transverse and spinous processes of vertebrae, occipital bone (Iliocostalis, Longissimus, Spinalis)
action: Bilaterally: extends vertebral column, fixes last rib. Unilaterally: laterally flexes to same side; assists rotation

name: Quadratus Lumborum
origin: Posterior iliac crest
insertion: Last (12th) rib; transverse processes of L1-L4
action: Bilaterally: extends lumbar spine, fixes last rib. Unilaterally: elevates hip, laterally flexes to same side

name: Gluteus Maximus
origin: Posterior ilium; posterior sacrum and coccyx; sacrotuberous ligament
insertion: Gluteal tuberosity of femur; iliotibial (IT) band
action: Hip extension; lateral rotation. Upper fibers: abduction. Lower fibers: adduction

name: Gluteus Medius
origin: External surface of ilium between posterior and anterior gluteal lines
insertion: Greater trochanter of femur (lateral surface)
action: Hip abduction; medial rotation. Stabilizes pelvis during walking

name: Gluteus Minimus
origin: External surface of ilium between anterior and inferior gluteal lines
insertion: Greater trochanter of femur (anterior surface)
action: Hip abduction; medial rotation. Assists in stabilizing the pelvis

name: Piriformis
origin: Anterior surface of sacrum (S2-S4); sacrotuberous ligament
insertion: Greater trochanter of femur (superior surface)
action: Lateral rotation of hip; abduction when hip is flexed. Sciatic nerve runs beneath or through it

name: Psoas / Iliopsoas
origin: Psoas: transverse processes and bodies of T12-L5. Iliacus: iliac fossa and anterior sacrum
insertion: Lesser trochanter of femur
action: Hip flexion; lateral rotation of femur. Bilateral: flexes trunk toward thigh

name: Tensor Fasciae Latae
origin: Anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS); anterior iliac crest
insertion: Iliotibial (IT) band to lateral condyle of tibia
action: Hip flexion, abduction, and medial rotation. Tenses IT band; assists knee extension

name: Tibialis Posterior
origin: Posterior tibia and fibula; interosseous membrane
insertion: Navicular tuberosity; cuneiforms; bases of metatarsals 2-4
action: Plantar flexion; inversion of foot. Primary support of medial longitudinal arch

name: Fibularis (Peroneus) Longus
origin: Head and upper 2/3 of lateral fibula
insertion: Base of 1st metatarsal; medial cuneiform (plantar surface)
action: Eversion of foot; plantar flexion. Supports transverse and lateral arches

name: Extensor Digitorum Longus
origin: Lateral condyle of tibia; anterior fibula; interosseous membrane
insertion: Dorsal surface of middle and distal phalanges of toes 2-5
action: Extension of toes 2-5; dorsiflexion of foot; eversion of foot

name: Extensor Digitorum (hand)
origin: Lateral epicondyle of humerus (common extensor tendon)
insertion: Dorsal expansion of fingers 2-5
action: Extension of fingers 2-5 at MCP, PIP and DIP joints; assists wrist extension

name: Flexor Carpi Radialis
origin: Medial epicondyle of humerus (common flexor tendon)
insertion: Base of 2nd and 3rd metacarpals
action: Wrist flexion; radial deviation of wrist

name: Adductor Magnus
origin: Inferior ramus of pubis; ramus of ischium; ischial tuberosity
insertion: Linea aspera of femur (adductor part); adductor tubercle (hamstring part)
action: Hip adduction. Adductor part: flexion and medial rotation. Hamstring part: extension and lateral rotation

name: Adductor Longus
origin: Pubic body just below pubic crest
insertion: Middle third of linea aspera of femur
action: Hip adduction; assists hip flexion and medial rotation

name: Adductor Brevis
origin: Inferior ramus of pubis
insertion: Proximal linea aspera of femur
action: Hip adduction; assists hip flexion and medial rotation

name: Pectineus
origin: Pectineal line of pubis
insertion: Pectineal line of femur just below lesser trochanter
action: Hip adduction; hip flexion; assists medial rotation

name: Gracilis
origin: Inferior ramus of pubis; body of pubis
insertion: Medial surface of proximal tibia (pes anserinus)
action: Hip adduction; knee flexion; medial rotation of tibia when knee is flexed
